docs.title: RPi Repair Reference
docs.text: A board-level repair reference for Raspberry Pi hardware. Component inventories, voltage maps, test points, and documented failure modes.

docs.title: What's Here
docs.text:
This site organizes repair-relevant data by Pi model and variant:

  • Components - Key ICs, regulators, and passives with designators and part numbers
  • Voltages - Expected voltage rails and measurements at key points
  • Test Points - Board test point locations and their expected readings
  • Failure Modes - Documented failure patterns, symptoms, and repair approaches

docs.title: Models Covered
docs.text:
Family Variants Pi 3 3B, 3B+, 3A+ Pi 4 Model B Pi 5 Model B

Pick a model from the navigation tabs above to get started.

docs.title: About
docs.text: This site is a community-maintained reference for Raspberry Pi board-level repair. The goal is to collect and organize hardware repair data that is currently scattered across forums, videos, and personal notes into a single searchable resource.

docs.title: Who
docs.text: This site is maintained by dpetzel and community contributors.

docs.title: Non-Affiliation Disclaimer
docs.text:
This site is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or associated with Raspberry Pi Ltd or the Raspberry Pi Foundation. "Raspberry Pi" is a trademark of Raspberry Pi Ltd.

All information is gathered from publicly available sources, datasheets, and hands-on repair experience.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions of repair data are welcome. This site improves when experienced repair technicians share what they know.

docs.title: What to Contribute
docs.text:
• Component identification data (IC designators, part numbers, packages)
  • Voltage measurements at test points (specify board revision and conditions)
  • Documented failure modes with symptoms and confirmed root causes
  • Corrections to existing data

docs.title: How to Submit
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository on GitHub
  2. Add or edit content in the docs/ directory
  3. Follow the existing page structure and formatting conventions
  4. Open a pull request with a description of what you added and how you verified it

docs.title: Data Quality Standards
docs.text:
• All voltage measurements must specify: board revision, measurement conditions (bare board vs. booted), and probe points
  • Component data should reference a source (datasheet, visual identification, schematic)
  • Failure modes should distinguish between confirmed root causes and suspected causes

docs.title: License
docs.text: By contributing, you agree that your contributions are licensed under the same terms as the rest of the site (MIT).

docs.title: Accuracy
docs.text: The information on this site is provided "as is" with no guarantee of accuracy or completeness. Hardware repair is complex and board revisions change over time. Always verify critical measurements against official documentation where available.

docs.title: Assumption of Risk
docs.text:
Board-level repair involves risk of further damage to hardware, electrical shock, and other hazards. By using information from this site, you accept full responsibility for any outcomes.

This is not professional repair advice. If you are not comfortable working with electronics at the component level, seek a qualified repair professional.

docs.title: No Warranty
docs.text: No warranty is provided, express or implied. The maintainers and contributors are not liable for any damage resulting from the use of information on this site.

docs.title: Affiliate Disclosure
docs.text:
This page will contain FTC-required affiliate disclosure once monetization is added to the site.

For now: this site does not currently contain affiliate links or paid endorsements. When affiliate links are added (e.g., links to component suppliers), they will be clearly identified and this page will be updated with full disclosure language.

docs.title: Guides
docs.text:
General-purpose repair technique guides. This section is planned for future content.

Planned topics:

  • Multimeter basics for board diagnosis
  • Hot air rework station techniques
  • Reading schematics for repair
  • Common SMD component identification
